summary: This notebook presents a trajectory prediction baseline for the NFL Big Data Bowl that combines a deterministic geometric baseline with a neural network learning residual corrections. It constructs a rich feature set including physics-based momentum, route clustering, GNN-lite neighbor embeddings, and temporal progress indicators. The core architecture is a GRU with multi-head attention that predicts cumulative displacement over a future horizon. By training on the difference between actual player movement and a geometry-derived endpoint, the model focuses on complex interactions like coverage and collisions rather than basic physics. The workflow demonstrates how domain-informed geometric priors can simplify the learning task and improve generalization in sports trajectory forecasting.
competition_flow: Loads weekly NFL player tracking CSVs, computes 167 hand-crafted geometric, spatial, and temporal features, trains a 5-fold GroupKFold GRU-Attention model on displacement residuals, and averages fold predictions to generate a field-clipped submission.
data_reading: pd.read_csv on weekly train/input_2023_w{w:02d}.csv and train/output_2023_w{w:02d}.csv files, concatenated into DataFrames
data_processing: Converts height strings to numeric inches/feet
Pads sequences to a fixed window of 10 frames
Fills missing values with group means or zeros
features_engineering: Physics-based momentum, kinetic energy, speed_squared, accel_magnitude
Ball-landing distance, angle, direction, and closing speed
Opponent proximity, closing speed, mirror WR offsets, pressure metrics
KMeans route clustering (7 clusters) on trajectory straightness/turns/speed
GNN-lite weighted neighbor embeddings (dx/dy/dvx/dvy for allies/opponents, counts, min/mean distances)
Temporal lags (1-5), rolling mean/std (3,5), deltas, EMAs
Time-progress ratios, frame_time, time_remaining, expected_x/y_at_ball, error_from_ball
13 geometric endpoint features: required velocity/acceleration, velocity error, alignment, urgency/coupling
models: JointSeqModel (2-layer GRU with 128 hidden dim, MultiheadAttention with 4 heads, Linear head outputting horizon*2 displacement)
loss_functions: TemporalHuber (Huber delta=0.5, exponential time decay weighting)
ensembling: Averages predictions across all 5 fold models, then clips final x/y coordinates to field boundaries before saving.
insights: Decomposing movement into a deterministic geometric baseline plus neural corrections reduces the learning burden and improves generalization.
GNN-lite weighted neighbor embeddings effectively capture spatial coupling between players without full graph computation.
Time-progress and ball-landing alignment features are critical for forecasting future trajectories in football.
